title: salvia verbenaca subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Salvia verbenaca subsp. ochroleuca, also known as yellow sage, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Salvia verbenaca. It is a plant that belongs to the mint family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and treat respiratory problems. Salvia verbenaca subsp. ochroleuca can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
